message:
fix(runtime): guard region drain against finalizer-spawned tasks

Finalizers running during FINALIZING state are allowed to spawn cleanup
tasks. Previously, asx_region_drain would proceed to seal the region
CLOSED even when a finalizer had spawned new tasks, leaving those tasks
orphaned. Now the drain checks task_count after the cleanup stack runs
and returns ASX_E_QUIESCENCE_TASKS_LIVE so the caller can drain again.

- Add post-cleanup task_count check in asx_region_drain
- Add cleanup_spawn_immediate_task helper for test setup
- Add region_drain_finalizer_spawned_task_requires_followup_drain test
  verifying two-phase drain when a finalizer spawns a task
- Update beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "bd-hwb.8", "title": "Implement adaptive-decision contract with expected-loss model, evidence ledger, and deterministic fallback", "description": "Background:
Plan section 6.11 requires adaptive choices to remain explainable and replay-safe.

Subtasks:
- Define expected-loss decision framework and allowed decision surfaces.
- Emit evidence records for adaptive choices and confidence inputs.
- Implement deterministic fallback path when confidence is insufficient.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Adaptive behavior is auditable and replay-compatible.
- Fallback path is always available and test-covered.
- Decision outputs are stable enough for regression monitoring.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:53:10.016326127Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-27T19:59:20.100244807Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-27T19:59:20.100217767Z", "close_reason": "done. Evidence: tests/unit/core/test_adaptive.c, tests/unit/evidence/test_evidence.c. Additional evidence: tests/e2e/e2e_core_lifecycle.c, tests/e2e/e2e_foundational_contracts.c, tests/invariant/lifecycle/test_lifecycle_legality.c; fixtures/rust_reference/, build/conformance/, .beads/issues.jsonl", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "bd-hwb.9", "title": "Implement security and safety posture checks for pointer ownership and invariant violation handling", "description": "Background:
Section 12 now includes explicit threat-model boundaries and production fault-containment expectations in addition to debug fail-fast behavior.

Subtasks:
- Document and enforce pointer ownership and stale-handle misuse defenses.
- Add misuse tests for illegal transitions, stale handles, unsafe pointer paths, and malformed-input boundaries.
- Codify in-scope vs out-of-scope threat boundaries for kernel responsibilities.
- Implement deterministic production containment path (region poison/isolation policy) so failures can be scoped without undefined behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Safety posture requirements are test-enforced and profile-aware.
- Failure behavior is deterministic, visible, and bounded by policy.
- Security-relevant misuse paths have regression coverage with reproducible artifacts.
- Threat boundary documentation maps directly to tests and explicit non-goals.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:53:10.119904097Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-27T06:57:17.513999481Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-27T06:57:17.513978090Z", "close_reason": "done. +{"id":"bd-irpy","title":"Region drain must not close with tasks spawned from finalizer cleanup","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T23:06:12.937003576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:11:05.422649406Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T23:11:05.422375908Z","close_reason":"Prevented finalizer-spawned tasks from being hidden by region close; added regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-ix8", "title": "EPIC: Build System, Portability Matrix, and Repository Scaffold", "description": "Background:
The runtime must compile and behave predictably across host and constrained targets. Build portability is a first-class requirement, not cleanup work.

Goal:
Establish build/repo infrastructure for GCC/Clang/MSVC plus embedded cross-target matrix, including QEMU validation harness.

Must preserve:
- dependency-free core assumptions
- profile-aware compile paths
- reproducible, diagnosable failures

Why this serves users:
- HFT teams need deterministic reproducible builds
- embedded teams need cross-target confidence before deployment
- maintainers need fast feedback loops with clear failure surfaces.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:47:41.970107711Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-27T19:52:13.326969139Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-27T19:52:13.326946788Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["build", "phase-2", "portability"]}
 {"id": "bd-ix8.1", "title": "Scaffold repository layout and module boundaries for include/src/tests/fixtures/tools/docs", "description": "Background:
The codebase structure must enforce architectural boundaries from day one to prevent accidental coupling.

Subtasks:
- Create canonical directory map for include, src, tests, fixtures, tools, docs.
- Define ownership for each module boundary and allowed dependencies.
- Define naming conventions for APIs, internal files, and test fixtures.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Layout aligns with proposed architecture and parity tracking needs.
- No ambiguous module ownership.
- New contributors can infer where code belongs without guessing.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:48:42.042079992Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-27T04:35:54.621282796Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-27T04:35:54.621264252Z", "close_reason": "Completed scaffold contract details: canonical directory map, explicit module ownership/dependency boundaries, and naming conventions in docs/PHASE2_SCAFFOLD_MANIFEST.md. Evidence: tests/e2e/e2e_core_lifecycle.c, tests/e2e/e2e_foundational_contracts.c. Unit test evidence: tests/unit/core/test_status.c, tests/unit/runtime/test_rt.c", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["build", "phase-2", "structure"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-ix8.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-296.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-ix8.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-296.30", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-ix8.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-ix8", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-ix8.2", "title": "Implement primary Makefile workflow and optional CMake generator strategy", "description": "Background:
Build and test commands must be stable and scriptable across environments.

Subtasks:
- Define make targets for build, lint, format-check, test, conformance, fuzz-smoke, profile-parity.
- Document optional CMake generation path for consumers needing IDE/toolchain integration.
- Define reproducibility expectations (flags, target profiles, deterministic options).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Make targets cover all quality gates in plan section 10.6.
- Commands are CI-compatible and locally reproducible.
- Optional CMake path does not become the primary maintenance burden.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:48:42.120183292Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-27T04:42:13.473721408Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-27T04:42:13.473702843Z", "close_reason": "done.
